canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, Minerva is consistently characterized as a benchmark for mathematical reasoning. It is defined as an instrument for assessing problem-solving ability specifically on scientific and mathematical questions. Across the surveyed papers, Minerva is used as part of a suite of evaluation tools to measure this capability, with performance reported as a percentage. It is frequently listed and used alongside other mathematical benchmarks such as MATH 500, Olympiad Bench, and AIME. The sole function of Minerva described in these sources is to operationalize and evaluate mathematical reasoning in models.
papers: GATEAU: Selecting Influential Samples for Long Context Alignment
MAVL: A Multilingual Audio-Video Lyrics Dataset for Animated Song Translation
Whisper-UT: A Unified Translation Framework for Speech and Text
